records.ety: [L. conveniens, -entis, suitable, p. pr. of convenire to be suitable, to come. See Convene, v. i.]

defs: Fit or adapted; suitable; proper; becoming; appropriate.
quotes: Feed me with food convenient for me.
Neither filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not convenient.

defs: Affording accommodation or advantage; well adapted to use; handly; as, a convenient house; convenient implements or tools.
Seasonable; timely; opportune; as, a convenient occasion; a convenient season.
Near at hand; easy of access.
quotes: Hereties used to be brought thither, convenient for burning.
